title: What is the Cost of Reading

content.problemStatement:
There is a universal library, where there is a big waiting room with seating capacity for maximum mmm people, each of whom completes reading nnn books sequentially. Reading each book requires one unit of time.

Unfortunately, reading service is provided sequentially. After all of the mmm people enter the library, the entrance gate is closed. There is only one reading table. So when someone reads, others have to wait in the waiting room.

At first everybody chooses nnn books they want to read. It takes xxx amount of time. People can choose books simultaneously. Then they enter the waiting room. After reading nnn books the person leaves the library immediately.

As nothing is free, the cost of reading is also not free. If a person stays in the library ttt units of time then the cost of reading is ⌊t−nm⌋\left \lfloor \frac{t-n}{m} \right \rfloor⌊mt−n​⌋ units of money. So, the ithi^{th}ith person pays for time xxx he needs to choose books and the time (i−1)∗n(i-1)*n(i−1)∗n he needs to wait for all the persons before him to complete reading.

Note: ⌊a⌋\left \lfloor a \right \rfloor⌊a⌋ denotes the floor(aaa).


Input Format

Each case contains three space-separated positive integers nnn, mmm and xxx where n,x≤1000n, x \leq 1000n,x≤1000 and m≤1015m \leq 10^{15}m≤1015.
End of input is determined by three zeros.
There are no more than 1000 test cases.


Output Format

For each case, output in a single line the total unit of money the library gets in that day.
content.inputFormat: The input consists of multiple test cases. Each test case contains three space-separated positive integers n, m, and x where n, x ≤ 1000 and m ≤ 10^15. The end of input is determined by three zeros.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the total unit of money the library gets in that day.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, n = 1, m = 100, and x = 9. The total cost is 9. For the second test case, n = 11, m = 2, and x = 10. The total cost is 15. For the third test case, n = 12, m = 2, and x = 11. The total cost is 16.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to calculate the cost of reading for each person and then sum up these costs. The cost of reading for the ith person is ⌊(t - n) / m⌋, where t is the total time the person spends in the library. The total time t is the sum of the time the person spends choosing books (x) and the time the person spends waiting for all the previous people to complete reading ((i - 1) * n). We can calculate the cost of reading for each person and then sum up these costs to get the total unit of money the library gets in that day.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * m) — where T is the number of test cases and m is the number of people in the library. However, since m is at most 10^15, we can consider it as a constant for the purpose of time complexity analysis. Therefore, the time complexity is O(T).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we only need a constant amount of space to store the input and the output.
